title: Age-Related Macular Degeneration OCT Grading (PWDR)
sub_domain: Macular layer + drusen + fluid recovery from OCT with AREDS / AMD-stage categorical readout
source_data_quality_note: v2 PWDR Principle. Wraps L1-042 OCT (existing in v1 genesis) with deterministic AREDS clinical-classification + Beckman 2013 staging rules to assign AMD-stage labels {none, early, intermediate, late_dry_GA, late_wet_neovascular}. Universal in ophthalmology. AMD is the leading cause of blindness in adults > 50 in developed countries. UTSW Ophthalmology retinal clinic high-volume use.

gate_substitutions.conservation_substitute: softmax simplex closure on AMD-stage ordinal {no_AMD, early, intermediate, late_dry_GA, late_wet_CNV}

discrete_readout.threshold_function: AMD-stage(OCT) = step on (drusen_size_um, drusen_count, RPE_pigmentary_changes, GA_area_mm2, intraretinal_fluid_presence, subretinal_fluid_presence, PED_height_um): no_AMD if no drusen; early if small_drusen <= 63 um with limited count; intermediate if intermediate_drusen 63-125 um OR large_drusen > 125 um OR pigmentary changes; late_dry_GA if geographic_atrophy_area > 0; late_wet_CNV if intraretinal_fluid OR subretinal_fluid OR pigment epithelial detachment with CNV signature. Per AREDS 2001 + Beckman 2013 clinical classification.
discrete_readout.underlying_physical_state: OCT B-scan layer segmentation (RPE, BM, ELM, ONL, OPL, INL, IPL, GCL) + drusen detection + fluid pocket detection + GA boundary segmentation + PED height measurement recovered from L1-042 OCT volume
discrete_readout.threshold_continuity_proof: Drusen size and GA area are continuous on layer-segmentation outputs; AMD-stage boundaries form measure-zero hyperplanes. Smoothed-classification globally Lipschitz with constant ~0.05 per percentage-point of segmentation error.

E.description: AMD OCT Grading (PWDR): wraps L1-042 OCT with AREDS 2001 + Beckman 2013 clinical classification rules. Stage 1 (analytical, from L1-042): from spectral-domain or swept-source OCT macular volume, segment retinal layers (10-layer RNFL/GCL/IPL/INL/OPL/ONL/ELM/IS-OS/RPE/BM); detect drusen (sub-RPE deposits); detect fluid pockets (intraretinal cysts, subretinal fluid, pigment epithelial detachment); segment geographic atrophy boundary. Stage 2 (deterministic threshold): apply AREDS / Beckman criteria for AMD staging. Difficulty tier delta = 3.
E.forward_model: Stage 1 (L1-042): OCT B-scan -> {layer_boundaries, drusen_mask, fluid_mask, GA_mask, PED_height}. Stage 2: AMD_stage = step(features per AREDS 2001 + Beckman 2013).
E.world_state_x: Per-eye AMD stage {no_AMD, early, intermediate, late_dry_GA, late_wet_CNV}.
E.observation_y: Macular OCT volume (typically 512x512x128, 6mm x 6mm x 2mm; SD-OCT 5-7 micrometer axial resolution).

G.dag: L.oct_acquisition -> L.layer_segmentation -> L.drusen_detection -> L.fluid_detection -> L.ga_segmentation -> L.amd_stage_classifier -> int.spatial
coupling_constraints: layer_to_lesion: retinal layer segmentation + drusen / fluid / GA detection share the OCT volume; coupled segmentation enforces topological consistency (drusen are sub-RPE, fluid is intraretinal or subretinal, GA is RPE-BM atrophy)

W.regime: Existence inherited from L1-042. Uniqueness conditional on adequate OCT image quality (signal strength index > 6/10 typical). Stability dominated by patient_motion + shadowing_artifact. Joint Hadamard well-posedness established by AREDS Research Group 2001 (foundational), Beckman 2013 (consensus classification), Schmidt-Erfurth 2018 (OCT in AMD review), De Fauw 2018 (DeepMind referrable disease classification benchmark).

C.solver_class: linear-operator + image-segmentation [layer + drusen + fluid + GA segmentation U-Net] + categorical-readout [AREDS / Beckman classifier] | end-to-end deep neural [DeepMind retinal AI, Heidelberg AnyDrusen]
C.error_bound: Inherits L1-042. Misclassification probability bound: Pr(stage_hat != stage_true) <= C * |feature_volumetric_error| / d(features, stage_threshold).
C.complexity: O(H * W * Z) for segmentation; O(1) for classification

recast_attempted.recast_path: Original: 'Classify OCT into AMD stages'. Recast: 'Segment retinal layers + drusen + fluid + GA via L1-042; apply AREDS / Beckman classification rules'. Feasible because retinal anatomy + AMD lesions have well-defined OCT forward model.

joint_well_posedness_references: Age-Related Eye Disease Study Research Group (2001) The Age-Related Eye Disease Study system for classifying age-related macular degeneration from stereoscopic color fundus photographs: AREDS Report No. 6. Am J Ophthalmol 132(5):668-681.
Ferris FL III et al. (2013) Clinical classification of age-related macular degeneration (Beckman Initiative). Ophthalmology 120(4):844-851.

Schmidt-Erfurth U, Klimscha S, Waldstein SM, Bogunovic H (2017) A view of the current and future role of optical coherence tomography in the management of age-related macular degeneration. Eye 31(1):26-44.
De Fauw J et al. (2018) Clinically applicable deep learning for diagnosis and referral in retinal disease. Nat Med 24:1342-1350.
Schmidt-Erfurth U et al. (2018) Artificial intelligence in retina. Prog Retin Eye Res 67:1-29.

distinctness_audit.rationale: L1-042 OCT recovers reflectivity volumes; L1-530 wraps with drusen + fluid + GA segmentation + AMD classifier. Different solution_space (1D AMD stage vs 3D reflectivity); adds L.layer_segmentation, L.drusen_detection, L.fluid_detection, L.ga_segmentation, L.amd_stage_classifier primitives.

v3_metadata.clinical_context: Universal in ophthalmology. AMD is the leading cause of blindness in adults > 50 in developed countries. AREDS / Beckman classification underpins anti-VEGF treatment decisions for wet AMD (ranibizumab, aflibercept, bevacizumab, brolucizumab, faricimab). UTSW Department of Ophthalmology retina clinic. FDA-cleared autonomous AI: DeepMind retinal AI (Moorfields collaboration), Heidelberg AnyDrusen, NotalVision Home OCT — all PWDR-shaped systems.
